name: Bitbucket API
what_it_does: Atlassian Bitbucket's REST API for managing Git repositories, pull requests, branches, pipelines (CI/CD), deployments, and code review workflows.
use_cases: Automating pull request creation, reviews, and merges
Triggering and monitoring Bitbucket Pipelines CI/CD
Managing repository settings, branch permissions, and webhooks
Syncing code review status with project management tools
Building custom code review workflows and quality gates
not_for: Teams using GitHub or GitLab (different platforms with different APIs)
Monorepo-heavy workflows where GitHub's features are more mature
Teams not in the Atlassian ecosystem (Jira/Confluence)
best_when: Your team uses Bitbucket for code hosting in the Atlassian ecosystem (Jira + Bitbucket) and needs to automate git operations or CI/CD.
avoid_when: You use GitHub, GitLab, or another VCS provider.

auth.notes: OAuth2 (recommended) for user-context operations. App passwords (API tokens) for server-to-server. Access tokens with specific scopes for repositories, pull requests, pipelines, etc.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, unlimited public repos, 50 build minutes/month
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing for small teams. Build minutes included per plan. JIRA integration is primary differentiator. Bitbucket Server (self-hosted) has separate licensing.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with type and detail fields. Errors are readable but can be vague for permission-related issues. Standard HTTP status codes used consistently.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Pull request creation with same content creates duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: API URLs require workspace slug, not just username — workspace and project are separate concepts
App passwords have different scopes from OAuth tokens — verify which scopes are needed
Bitbucket Cloud and Bitbucket Server/Data Center have completely different APIs
Pipelines API requires enabling Bitbucket Pipelines in repository settings first
Merge commits and pull request state require understanding Bitbucket's branching model
Rate limits per user token — service accounts for automation have the same limits as users

security_score_components.security_notes: Atlassian Bitbucket. OAuth2 or app passwords. Fine-grained permission scopes for repos, PRs, pipelines. Workspace admin credentials are high-value — use least privilege.
